title: salix tenuijulis var. subintegrifolia
intro: Salix tenuijulis var. subintegrifolia, also known as the narrowleaf willow or coastal willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to coastal areas of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Salix tenuijulis var. subintegrifolia include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource for herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
